title: Modals of Possibility: Solving Body Language
description: Master the modals of possibility (might, may, could) and contrast them with certainty (can't, must) (Unit 9: Body talk).

questions.text:
### Modals of Possibility & Certainty

When we are not 100% sure about a situation, we use modals to speculate:

1. **Possibility (50% sure)**: We use **might**, **may**, or **could** + base verb.
   * *Example*: He is looking at his phone. He **might be** lost.
   * *Example*: It **may rain** later, so bring an umbrella.
2. **Negative Possibility**: Use **might not** or **may not** (do not use *could not* for possibility, as *couldn't* means inability/impossibility).
   * *Example*: She **might not come** to the party (it is possible she won't).
3. **Certainty (90-100% sure)**:
   * **Must**: When we are sure something is true.
     * *Example*: She has a big smile. She **must be** very happy.
   * **Can't**: When we are sure something is impossible.
     * *Example*: He is yawning. He **can't be** fully awake.

questions.question: Which modal is best when you are 90% sure that something is impossible? 'He is wearing a heavy winter coat in summer. He ______ feel very comfortable.'
questions.hint: We use 'can't' to express that something is logically impossible.

questions.question: Choose the correct modal to complete the body language speculations:

statements.text: Sarah is biting her nails. She [might be] nervous about the exam.
He has won a trip to New York! He [must be] thrilled!
The wedding guests are laughing. The ceremony [can't be] boring.

questions.question: Complete the discussion about a mysterious wedding guest by writing might, must, or can't:
questions.text: A stranger is standing in the corner of the wedding hall. He [must] (must/can't) be a guest because he is wearing a very elegant suit. However, he is not talking to anyone. He [might|could] (might/must) feel lonely or shy. Look, he is looking at his watch again. He [might|may] (might/can't) want to leave early. He is not eating any food, so he [can't] (can't/must) be very hungry. Let's go over and talk to him; he [could|might] (could/must) be friendly!

questions.question: Match the clues in body language or situations to the logical speculations:

pairs.leftText: His arms are crossed and he is frowning.
She is shivering and rubbing her hands.
They are shaking hands and smiling.
pairs.rightText: They could be making a deal.

questions.question: Rearrange the words to form a speculation sentence about a situation:
Imagine your friend is yawning during a class presentation. Write 2 sentences speculating about why they are yawning. Use 'might' in one sentence and 'must' or 'can't' in the other.
